zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Vere於大約1336年出生在英國奧可福爾奧可福德時，她的父親John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford25歲，母親Maud de Badlesmere30歲。她於1359年與Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1398年6月15日享年63歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Vere于大约1336年出生在英国奧可福爾奧可福德时，她的父亲John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford25岁，母亲Maud de Badlesmere30岁。她于1359年与Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1398年6月15日享年63岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, mostly from Vair in Ancenis, named with Gaulish ver(n)- ‘alder’. This was the probable home in the 11th century of the Breton lord Aubrey (Albericus) de Ver, who followed William the Conqueror to England and founded an influential family who held the earldom of Oxford until 1703. In a few cases the surname could be from other places in France, such as Ver in Manche and Calvados, which have the same etymology as Vair.
French (mainy Vendée; Veré): variant of Vairé, from Old French vairie ‘variegated, spotted’, applied as a nickname for someone who has freckles.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Vere was born about 1336, in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, her father, John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford, was 25 and her mother, Maud de Badlesmere, was 30. She married Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont in 1359. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 15 June 1398, at the age of 63, and was buried in London, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Vere est née environ 1336, à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Maud de Badlesmere, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont en 1359. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 15 juin 1398, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Vere um 1336 in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford 25 und ihre Mutter Maud de Badlesmere 30. Sie hat 1359 Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 15. Juni 1398 im Alter von 63 Jahren und wurde in London, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Vere è nata il circa 1336 nel Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, sua padre, John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Maud de Badlesmere, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont nel 1359. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 15 giugno 1398, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolta a London, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Vereが約1336年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアオクスフォードで生まれた時，父John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxfordは25歳，母Maud de Badlesmereは30歳であった。1359年にHenry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1398年6月15日に63歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Vere(이)가 대략 1336년 영국 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Badlesmere(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1359년 Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1398년 6월 15일 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던에 묻혔다.

mn.Penshurst, Ospringe, Kent, England: Penshurst, Centlond, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Vere nasceu aproximadamente 1336, em Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Badlesmere, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont em 1359. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 15 de junho de 1398, com 63 anos, e foi sepultada em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Vere родилась около 1336 г. в местности оксфорд, оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Badlesmere, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont в 1359 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 15 июня 1398 г. в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Vere nació aproximadamente 1336, en Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Maud de Badlesmere, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Henry de Beaumont, 3rd Baron Beaumont en 1359. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 15 de junio de 1398, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultada en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
